Notice Of Motion To Consolidate Claims
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Paul Nathan, Tel. No. 1-415-341-1144 appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Adolpho Karajah of KSM Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-474-1900 appearing for the
Defendant Berhe & De Soto Cab;
Atty. Alexander MacDougall of the Law Office of Catherine Walsh, Tel. No. 1-925-
825-9500 ext. 42 appearing for the Defendant Geico by Telephone Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Plaintiff Carissa Copeland's motion to consolidate claims is granted and the trial date for all three consolidated claims is now May 13, 2019. This case is now consolidated with the two UIM cases (Copeland v. Allstate Northbrook Insurance Company #0352754659TGD and Copeland v. GEICO #0495428040101011) for all purposes. Ms. Copeland may file an amended complaint alleging all three claims in this case. Ms. Copeland has shown that there is likely to be substantial overlap of the evidence in this case and the two UIM cases warranting the consolidation of all three. The interests of judicial economy and avoidance of possible inconsistent results outweighs the insurers' interests in having the UIM claims adjudicated by arbitration and the interests of the defendants in this case in having the claim against them tried separately from the UIM claims. Per the stipulation of the parties to this case, the trial date is continued to May 13, 2019 and all discovery deadlines are triggered off the new trial date. Additionaly, the court orders that this matter automatically return to binding arbitration for Uninsured Motorist Claims should the current Defendants Behre & De Soto Cab Co. is dismissed from this action. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare form of order and to comply wth CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Consolidate Claims
Off Calendar Jun-13-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Adolph Karajahm, Esq. 415-474-1900 of Kern Noda Devine & Segal for Defendant Mogos Zeregaber Berhe.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARISSA COPELAND's Motion To Consolidate Claims.

Plaintiff Carissa Copeland's motion to consolidate this case with two uninsured motorist arbitrations is denied. While consolidation of court litigation with an uninsured motorist arbitration is permissible, Ms. Copeland failed to provide admissible evidence that this case has a significant relationship with the two uninsured motorist arbitrations to warrant denial of the insurers' contractual right to have the uninsured motorist claims determined by arbitration. The only evidence submitted by Ms. Copeland regarding the relationship between this case and the two uninsured motorist arbitrations is her counsel's belief based on the hearsay statement of an unnamed "expert consultant" that "all the injuries Plaintiff suffered stemmed from the 2014 and 2015 Collisions."

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
